summary: Joel Spolsky announces planning for the next series of Stack Overflow DevDays conferences scheduled for fall 2011. The two-day conferences focus on providing intensive introductions to emerging technologies that developers want to learn. He references the original DevDays format of high-bandwidth sessions on topics like iPhone development, Python, and jQuery. For the 2011 edition, the team is crowdsourcing topic suggestions from the community, with early interest in DVCS, HTML5, and Node.js.
key_points: Stack Overflow DevDays conferences are returning as a series of two-day events in fall 2011
The conference format focuses on intensive introductions to technologies developers want to learn but don't yet use professionally
Topic selection is being crowdsourced from the Stack Overflow community via Meta Stack Overflow
Early community interest centers on DVCS, HTML5, and Node.js as top requested topics
money_quotes: The idea for the original DevDays was to have high-bandwidth, intensive introductions to a wide variety of new technologies.
The kinds of technologies that everybody wants to learn but doesn't necessarily need to use on a project right now.
key_insight: The best developer conferences focus on technologies people want to learn but haven't yet needed professionally, and letting the community choose topics ensures relevance.
